task_completion.description: JStock application is opened to view the data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible on the screen, showing either the portfolio or watchlist tabs.

task_completion.description: Portfolio summary header and metadata are correct
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text file named portfolio_summary.txt is visible, containing the 'PORTFOLIO SUMMARY' header, 'Portfolio Name: My Portfolio', and 'Number of Stocks: 3'.

task_completion.description: Portfolio stock details are accurately extracted and formatted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the stock details formatted exactly as '- <SYMBOL>: <UNITS> shares at $<PURCHASE_PRICE> = $<NET_PURCHASE_VALUE>' for AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA. The values must match the privileged info.

task_completion.description: Total net purchase value is correctly calculated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the line 'Total Net Purchase Value: $52627.5'.

task_completion.description: Watchlist summary is accurately extracted and formatted
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the 'WATCHLIST SUMMARY' header, 'Watchlist Name: My Watchlist', 'Number of Stocks: 5', and 'Stock Symbols: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA' (all 5 symbols must be present).

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock GUI to view the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The JStock graphical user interface is visible in the screenshots. The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly reading the backend CSV files (e.g., ~/.jstock/...) using terminal commands.

integrity.description: The output is saved to the correct file path
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is saved to a file named portfolio_summary.txt in the home directory (~/), rather than just being printed to the terminal or left in an unsaved text editor buffer.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the actual values rather than hallucinating
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the text file match the privileged info and the data visible in the JStock application screenshots, proving the agent didn't just generate a mock summary.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: /home/ga/portfolio_summary.txt. Portfolio count: 3. Portfolio stocks: AAPL (100 shares at $185.2 = $18520.0), MSFT (50 shares at $374.5 = $18725.0), NVDA (25 shares at $615.3 = $15382.5). Total Net Purchase Value: $52627.5. Watchlist count: 5. Watchlist stocks: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA.
